name: drift_immune_label_anchor
title: Anchor sibling-function branch targets via insert_label so they survive future label renumbering
summary:
When a regfix rule needs to redirect a branch target in a sibling function (e.g., `subst "\.L\d+" ".L412-4" @ 22`), the hardcoded `.L<N>` number drifts every time another function in the same .c file changes. Each integration creates a new label-arithmetic conflict cycle.

Fix: anchor the target with a custom `.LfuncNAME_target:` label inside the sibling function. The custom label name is stable -- only its @-index drifts (and only if THAT sibling itself is modified, which is the much rarer case).
proof: func_80049C24 (commit 6bdc29d), func_8004A1FC (commit c4bba60), func_80072CD4 (commit 9abed76) -- all three were stuck on label-arithmetic cascades that the deep-retry workers fixed by switching to insert_label anchors.

structural: regfix.txt has rules of form `func_X: subst "\\.L\\d+" ".L<N>[+-]<offset>" @ <idx>` where `func_X` is NOT the function being matched
fix-label-drift keeps updating the label number on each integration; after several integrations the rule no longer matches the right position

rule_skeleton.comment: anchor target inside the sibling function
rule_skeleton.label: .LfuncNAME_target:

rule_skeleton.comment: redirect branch in calling site to the anchored label

notes: Pick a TARGET_IDX that is INSIDE the sibling's body and stable (e.g., right before a known instruction, not at idx 0).
Use a unique custom name (`.Lfunc_XX_target` or `.LfuncBD30_target`) so multiple sibling fixes don't collide.
Audit found this pattern saved 3+ matches that had been stuck for hours on label-arithmetic cycles.
